frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Yaar, aajkal kuch girls office aise aati hain ki asli chehra samajh hi nahi aata. Simple aur natural rehna bhi theek hai, lekin bilkul apna khayal na rakhna bhi accha nahi lagta. Professional dikho, par itna makeup karke doosron ko impress karne ki kya zarurat hai?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: criticizing 'too much' makeup as deceptive while also expecting women to maintain their looks. It encodes the natural-vs-artificial standard and assumes makeup is for impressing others rather than for oneself.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
